title: Mixture of Sequence: Theme-Aware Mixture-of-Experts for Long-Sequence Recommendation
container-title: Proceedings of the ACM Web Conference 2026

publisher: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE)
title: Xami: Expert-Aware Adaptive Compression for Mixture-of-Experts on Sparse Tensor Cores

abstract: <jats:p>Recently, recommendation systems have actively integrated Transformers to capture real-time context. However, these systems often suffer from generalization imbalance, where predictions are biased toward popular (head) items due to the sparsity and volatility inherent in session-based data. To address this challenge, this paper proposes MoE-SLMRec, a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE)-based recommendation model that selects expert networks based on session-level contextual information. The proposed model extracts a session latent representation, h, through a session-aware controller and forms balanced predictive characteristics across the entire data distribution via dynamic routing. Experimental results demonstrate that MoE-SLMRec significantly outperforms the baseline SLMRec, improving accuracy by 1.51 percentage points (from 18.76% to 20.27%). Furthermore, the model achieved state-of-the-art performance in Recall@20 (0.8358) and MRR@20 (0.3455), validating simultaneous improvements in both retrieval capability and ranking quality. Notably, the model effectively stabilized the performance for head items while coordinating the generalization trade-off between head and tail segments. By ensuring a favorable capacity–cost trade-off while maintaining robust performance, this study presents a promising alternative under session-based recommendation settings, facilitating scalable deployment in real-time recommendation services.</jats:p>
title: A Mixture-of-Experts Model for Improved Generalization in Session-Aware Recommendation

title: InMoE: Interaction-Aware Graph Mixture of Experts for Trajectory Prediction

abstract: <jats:p>Isolated sign language recognition remains challenging due to subtle motion patterns and increased ambiguity in large-vocabulary settings. We propose the Reliability-Aware Modality-Adaptive Mixture-of-Experts (RMA-MoE) framework that dynamically exploits complementary modalities based on prediction confidence. Specifically, a dual-gating mechanism jointly controls expert activation and contribution across skeleton, RGB, and depth modalities. The proposed method improves performance by selectively activating other modalities only when the primary expert is unreliable, in contrast to traditional multimodal fusion. Experiments on the ViSL120 dataset show that RMA-MoE increases top-1 accuracy by 9.78% over the best unimodal baseline and yields consistent gains in challenging scenarios.</jats:p>
title: Reliability-Aware Modality-Adaptive Mixture-of-Experts for Isolated Vietnamese Sign Language Recognition
